nodes.context: Italian-American Mafia family operating in New York metropolitan area.
A group within the Lucchese crime family controlling activities in Brooklyn and Staten Island.
A crew within the Lucchese family based in Canarsie and Flatlands, Brooklyn.
A crew within the Lucchese family based in Bensonhurst, Brooklyn.
Semi-independent crew led by Jimmy Burke, associated with the Lucchese family.
One of the Five Families of New York; ally of the Lucchese family Brooklyn faction.
One of the Five Families of New York; ally of the Lucchese family Brooklyn faction.
One of the Five Families of New York; ally of the Lucchese family Brooklyn faction.
One of the Five Families of New York; ally of the Lucchese family Brooklyn faction.
Italian-American Mafia family based in New York City, with a Brooklyn faction.
A group within the Lucchese crime family controlling activities in Brooklyn and Staten Island, formed by merging the Vario Crew and the Bensonhurst Crew.
A crew within the Lucchese family based in Canarsie and Flatlands, Brooklyn, led by Paul Vario and later others.
A crew within the Lucchese family based in Bensonhurst, Brooklyn, merged with the Vario Crew to form the Brooklyn faction.
Another New York Mafia family; mentioned in relation to a member's half-brother.

nodes.standard_name: Lucchese crime family New Jersey faction
nodes.context: A faction of the Lucchese family operating in New Jersey.

edges.evidence_quote: The Lucchese crime family's Brooklyn faction is a group of Italian-American mobsters within the Lucchese crime family
edges.context: The Brooklyn faction is a part of the Lucchese crime family.

edges.evidence_quote: The merger occurred during the early 2000s, when the Lucchese family administration decided that Vario Crew Caporegime Domenico "Danny" Cutaia would also take control of the Bensonhurst Crew.
edges.context: The Vario Crew and Bensonhurst Crew were merged to form the Brooklyn faction.

edges.evidence_quote: The crew was controlled by Caporegime Paul Vario during the early 1960s into the early 1980s
edges.context: The Vario Crew is a crew within the Lucchese crime family.

edges.evidence_quote: The 19th Hole Crew , sometimes called Bensonhurst Crew , was based in Bensonhurst, Brooklyn , before it was merged into the Brooklyn faction.
edges.context: The Bensonhurst Crew is a crew within the Lucchese crime family.

edges.evidence_quote: Although Burke's crew was independent, many members were associates of the Lucchese family through Burke's and Vario's longtime friendship.
edges.context: The Robert's Lounge Crew was associated with the Lucchese family through Burke's friendship with Vario.

edges.evidence_quote: Burke led his semi-independent Robert's Lounge Crew , from his bar Robert's Lounge . The Robert's Lounge crew was composed of numerous people who were involved in armed robbery, hijacking, and murder. Although Burke's crew was independent, many members were associates of the Lucchese family through Burke's and Vario's longtime friendship.
edges.context: The Robert's Lounge Crew cooperated with the Vario Crew through the friendship between Burke and Vario.

edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Gambino , Genovese , Bonanno and Colombo crime families
edges.context: The Brooklyn faction is allied with the Gambino crime family.

edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Gambino , Genovese , Bonanno and Colombo crime families
edges.context: The Brooklyn faction is allied with the Genovese crime family.

edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Gambino , Genovese , Bonanno and Colombo crime families
edges.context: The Brooklyn faction is allied with the Bonanno crime family.

edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Gambino , Genovese , Bonanno and Colombo crime families
edges.context: The Brooklyn faction is allied with the Colombo crime family.

edges.evidence_quote: The Lucchese crime family's Brooklyn faction is a group of Italian-American mobsters within the Lucchese crime family
edges.context: The Brooklyn faction is a sub-unit of the Lucchese crime family.

edges.evidence_quote: the Vario Crew ... merged together ... the Lucchese Borgata referred to this crew as the Brooklyn faction
edges.context: The Brooklyn faction was formed by merging the Vario Crew and the Bensonhurst Crew.

edges.evidence_quote: the Vario Crew ... merged together ... the Lucchese Borgata referred to this crew as the Brooklyn faction
edges.context: The Brooklyn faction was formed by merging the Vario Crew and the Bensonhurst Crew.

edges.context: The two crews were merged to form the Brooklyn faction.

edges.target: Lucchese crime family New Jersey faction
edges.evidence_quote: Joseph M. 'Little Joe' Perna ... former member of the New Jersey faction, before joining the Brooklyn faction ... Transferred back to the New Jersey faction
edges.context: A member transferred between the Brooklyn faction and the New Jersey faction.
